name: Chestnut Soup with Amaretto
recipeInstructions:
1. Make a slash in the rounded side of each chestnut and roast in a pre-heated 400°F oven for 5 minutes.
2. Peel off the shell and inner skin of the roasted chestnuts.
3. Peel the carrots, parsnip, onion, and garlic. Finely chop all of the vegetables, including the scallions and celery.
4. Melt the butter in a large saucepan over medium heat.
5. Add the chopped vegetables to the saucepan and cook for five minutes, or until soft but not brown.
6. Add the roasted chestnuts, brown stock, and bouquet garni to the saucepan.
7. Gently simmer the soup for 40 minutes, or until the chestnuts are very soft.
8. Remove the bouquet garni from the soup.
9. Puree the soup in a blender or food processor.
10. Return the pureed soup to the saucepan. Stir in the amaretto liqueur, two-thirds of the cream, and season with salt, black pepper, cayenne pepper, and nutmeg to taste. Adjust seasoning as needed for flavor.
11. To serve, ladle the chestnut soup into warmed bowls. Pour a small amount of remaining cream into each bowl and swirl it into the soup, stirring gently with a skewer.
12. Serve the chestnut soup immediately while hot. The dish is rich, so keep other elements of the meal simple.
